title: AI Transformation Architect
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: How to connect AI initiatives to business strategy and goals.
knowledge.K2: Methods to discover and prioritise AI opportunities across departments by value and feasibility.
knowledge.K3: How to build a business case and estimate return on investment for AI.
knowledge.K4: AI operating models: ownership, funding, standards and central enablement.
knowledge.K5: Data foundations required for AI: availability, quality, access and governance.
knowledge.K6: Platform and architecture choices including build versus buy and model swappability.
knowledge.K7: AI governance, risk and responsible-AI guardrails aligned to regulation.
knowledge.K8: Change management and skills needed for organisation-wide adoption.
knowledge.K9: How to pilot and then scale AI solutions safely.
knowledge.K10: How to measure portfolio impact across adoption, outcomes, cost and risk.
knowledge.K11: The role of embedded champions in accelerating adoption.
knowledge.K12: Emerging AI trends and their strategic implications.

skills.S1: Translate strategy into a small set of high-value AI ambitions.
skills.S2: Run structured discovery and prioritise an opportunity portfolio.
skills.S3: Build quantified business cases with defined success metrics.
skills.S4: Design an AI operating model with clear roles and decision rights.
skills.S5: Assess and plan data foundations alongside use cases.
skills.S6: Make build-versus-buy and platform decisions for interoperability and security.
skills.S7: Define governance, acceptable-use and oversight policies.
skills.S8: Plan and lead change, communication and skills programmes.
skills.S9: Run pilots with clear criteria and scale what works.
skills.S10: Track and report portfolio adoption, outcomes, cost and risk.
skills.S11: Enable and support embedded AI champions.
skills.S12: Horizon-scan and adjust strategy to emerging AI developments.

behaviours.B1: Leads with initiative and strategic curiosity.
behaviours.B2: Takes responsibility for sustainable, well-governed outcomes.
behaviours.B3: Collaborates across business and technical stakeholders.
behaviours.B4: Acts with integrity on ethics, governance and regulation.
behaviours.B5: Operates confidently amid ambiguity and organisational complexity.

Duty 1.statement: Align AI initiatives with business strategy and prioritise a portfolio.

Duty 2.statement: Build business cases and define ROI for AI investment.

Duty 3.statement: Design the AI operating model and data foundations.

Duty 4.statement: Choose platforms and architecture and set governance.

Duty 5.statement: Lead change, skills and adoption with embedded champions.

Duty 6.statement: Pilot, scale, measure portfolio impact and stay current.
